title: Thousands of Upstate NY businesses face utility shutoffs sparked by hefty power bills
summary: Upstate NY businesses face significant utility shutoff risk due to unpaid National Grid bills, with January-February 2026 showing a threefold increase in at-risk customers compared to the prior year. This economic hardship trend may signal broader business cash flow challenges affecting the regional economy and potentially tax revenue generation for Syracuse.

political_signal.description: Growing business utility shutoff crisis indicates emerging constituent pressure regarding utility affordability and business sustainability; potential advocacy trigger for city leadership intervention or state regulatory review.

processing_notes: Article location (Baldwinsville) is outside Syracuse city limits but affects Upstate NY regional economy
No dollar amounts specified in excerpt; full article may contain additional financial data
State-level utility regulation issue; CIO should flag for mayor/economic development coordination
